content.text:
You are classifying files for an AI-first external drive.

For each file object in the input JSON array, return a JSON array with one object per file.
Return valid JSON only. No markdown. No prose.

Each output object must contain:
- rel_path
- title
- content_kind
- domain
- lifecycle
- confidence
- tags: array of short strings
- summary: one sentence
- entities: array of objects with fields name and type
- relations: array of objects with fields from, relation, to
- recommended_zone
- canonical_priority: one of low, medium, high

Guidance:
- Infer semantics from path, filename, extension, and sampled text.
- Use practical domains like ai, security, infra, career, health, research, finance, media, prompt_engineering.
- Use lifecycle values only from: inbox, active, reference, archive, system.
- Keep entities and relations conservative; do not invent specifics unsupported by the input.
- Favor retrieval usefulness over literary summaries.
- Use recommended_zone only from: 00_inbox, 10_active, 20_reference, 30_archive, 40_system.
- Prefer 20_reference for documents meant to be reused, 10_active for live workspaces, 30_archive for dated snapshots and exports, 40_system for manifests/config/state, 00_inbox for ambiguous intake.

Input JSON:
[{"rel_path": "20_reference/assets/.files/GenZ_Black_LGBTQ_Male_Intersectional_Analysis_Report.pdf", "name": "GenZ_Black_LGBTQ_Male_Intersectional_Analysis_Report.pdf", "extension": ".pdf", "mime_type": "application/pdf", "size_bytes": 835864, "zone": "20_reference", "sample_text": ""}]

messages.content:
```json
[
  {
    "rel_path": "20_reference/assets/.files/GenZ_Black_LGBTQ_Male_Intersectional_Analysis_Report.pdf",
    "title": "GenZ Black LGBTQ Male Intersectional Analysis Report",
    "content_kind": "report",
    "domain": "sociology",
    "lifecycle": "reference",
    "confidence": 0.9,
    "tags": [
      "GenZ",
      "Black",
      "LGBTQ",
      "male",
      "intersectionality",
      "analysis",
      "report",
      "social_science",
      "diversity",
      "equity",
      "inclusion"
    ],
    "summary": "This document presents an intersectional analysis report focusing on the experiences and characteristics of GenZ Black LGBTQ males.",
    "entities": [
      {
        "name": "GenZ",
        "type": "demographic"
      },
      {
        "name": "Black",
        "type": "demographic"
      },
      {
        "name": "LGBTQ",
        "type": "demographic"
      },
      {
        "name": "Male",
        "type": "demographic"
      },
      {
        "name": "Intersectional Analysis",
        "type": "research_method"
      }
    ],
    "relations": [],
    "recommended_zone": "20_reference",
    "canonical_priority": "high"
  }
]
```

thoughts.subject: Assessing PDF Attributes
thoughts.description: I'm currently focused on the PDF classification task, with the `name` and `rel_path` fields being the most valuable due to the absence of `sample_text`. I'm evaluating how to best utilize these two attributes to perform accurate classification, keeping in mind the structure of the data provided.

thoughts.subject: Deciphering Data Structure
thoughts.description: I've been analyzing the classification requirements, and I've started mapping the available attributes to the desired output fields. Since `sample_text` is missing, I'll be heavily dependent on `name` and `rel_path` for most of the classification tasks. I'm focusing on deriving `title`, `content_kind`, `domain`, `lifecycle`, and `tags` using a combination of string manipulation and keyword extraction from the file name.

thoughts.subject: Evaluating Document Components
thoughts.description: I've outlined the classification criteria, focusing on the available attributes and how to best extract the required outputs. Title extraction is straightforward from the filename. Content kind and lifecycle are easily inferred. Domain identification relies on name keywords. My current focus is on building a comprehensive set of tags and extracting relevant entities for this sample document.
